2603.22735.title: Explanation Generation for Contradiction Reconciliation with LLMs
2603.22735.paper_summary: Existing NLP work commonly treats contradictions as errors to be resolved by choosing which statements to accept or discard. Yet a key aspect of human reasoning in social interactions and professional domains is the ability to hypothesize explanations that reconcile contradictions. For example, "Cassie hates coffee" and "She buys coffee everyday" may appear contradictory, yet both are compatible if Cassie has the unenviable daily chore of buying coffee for all her coworkers. Despite the growing reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs), their ability to hypothesize such reconciliatory explanations remains largely unexplored. To address this gap, we introduce the task of reconciliatory explanation generation, where models must generate explanations that effectively render contradictory statements compatible. We propose a novel method of repurposing existing natural language inference (NLI) datasets, and introduce quality metrics that enable scalable automatic evaluation. Experiments with 18 LLMs show that most models achieve limited success in this task, and that the benefit of extending test-time compute by "thinking" plateaus as model size increases. Our results highlight an under-explored dimension of LLM reasoning and the need to address this limitation in enhancing LLMs' downstream applications such as chatbots and scientific aids.
2603.22735.paper_summary_zh: 現有的自然語言處理（NLP）研究通常將矛盾視為需要通過選擇接受或拒絕哪些陳述來解決的錯誤。<lb/>然而，人類在社交互動和專業領域中的推理一個關鍵方面是能夠假設能夠調和矛盾的解釋。<lb/>例如，「Cassie 討厭咖啡」和「她每天都買咖啡」看似矛盾，然而如果 Cassie 每天都要為她的同事們購買咖啡，這兩者實際上是兼容的。<lb/>儘管大型語言模型（LLMs）的推理能力不斷增強，但它們假設這種調和性解釋的能力仍然在很大程度上未被探索。<lb/>為了填補這一空白，我們引入了調和解釋生成的任務，模型必須生成有效使矛盾陳述兼容的解釋。<lb/>我們提出了一種重新利用現有自然語言推理（NLI）數據集的新方法，並引入了能夠進行可擴展自動評估的質量指標。<lb/>對 18 個 LLM 的實驗表明，大多數模型在這一任務中的成功有限，並且隨著模型大小的增加，通過「思考」來擴展測試時間計算的好處達到平臺。<lb/>我們的結果突顯了 LLM 推理的一個未被充分探索的維度，以及在增強 LLM 的下游應用（如聊天機器人和科學輔助工具）中解決這一限制的必要性。
2603.22735.author: Jason Chan et.al.
2603.22735.authors: Jason Chan, Zhixue Zhao, Robert Gaizauskas
